review_questions: Rerun or independently reproduce the submitted type-safety counts.
Inspect whether reductions came from meaningful narrowing and boundary typing, not superficial syntax churn.
Determine which allowed target the team is claiming: total bundle reduction or initial-load/code-splitting reduction.
Verify before/after bundle artifacts were produced under comparable build conditions.
Verify P95 improvements on at least two endpoints under identical data volume, concurrency, hardware, and bypass/rate-limit conditions.
Check whether excluded or failed benchmark artifacts change the conclusion.
Verify the measured flow satisfies the Week 4 database-query target.
Check whether EXPLAIN/query-count evidence compares like with like.
Inspect whether the new tests are meaningful regression tests, not page-load or superficial assertions.
Check flake/RCA claims when the team uses flake-fix path instead of new-test path.
Inspect the three claimed error-handling fixes and confirm at least one addresses real user-facing data loss or confusion.
Check before/after reproduction steps or screenshots.
Determine whether the team chose Lighthouse improvement or Critical/Serious axe closeout.
Verify scope covers the top pages claimed and does not overstate manual screen-reader/keyboard coverage.
Run or inspect the security probe tool and confirm it covers all four required attack surfaces.
Verify at least two vulnerability fixes with before/after proof and no broken tests.

impressions: Impression: notification-related work may be a reusable pattern worth inspecting.
Impression: accessibility work appears to use recognizable tools.
Impression: Playwright appears in the submission and may indicate runnable regression work.
not_independently_verified: chosen target path satisfies the spec when multiple target paths exist
security probe actually runs against a fresh instance
recent_commits: e5fed89 Enhance Reviewer Guide and FleetGraph Demo Setup
strengths: Broad audit language covers most Week 4 categories.
Structured submission ledger found with 8 proven categories.
Strong before/after and measurement vocabulary appears across submission evidence.
Category-specific evidence files appear for every required Week 4 category.
Security probe language appears to cover several required attack surfaces.
Repo includes a visible test surface for regression protection.

caveats: ESLint warning counts are useful as a worklist but are not the official before/after denominator.
Replacing any with unknown without meaningful narrowing is not counted as a real improvement.

Untyped parameters inferred as any are measured by `pnpm type-safety:report` and remain follow-up work; the official closeout claim is the source-required 25% reduction in counted type-safety syntax plus passing tests.
summaries.title: Type Safety

caveats: Total JS/CSS remains above baseline; the proven claim is limited to initial entry/code-splitting reduction. Lazy lowlight (2026-05-23) reduced largest chunk and total JS/CSS vs the prior closeout artifact without changing the claim ID.
summaries.title: Bundle Size

caveats: The benchmark intentionally bypasses API rate limiting in non-production to measure endpoint latency rather than limiter behavior.
The before benchmark was produced from isolated ref 7d31add with only the benchmark bypass patch applied.
The large-fixture current benchmark is supporting evidence only until the same fixture is benchmarked against the before ref.
Category 8 remains excluded and open; this Category 3 evidence does not claim security completion.
summaries.title: API Response Time

caveats: Bootstrap query-count proof is flow-level app-shell consolidation, not a row-level N+1 fix.
The before/after EXPLAIN artifact compares old fanout constituent SQL with current bootstrap constituent SQL on the current schema; it is not a historical-checkout EXPLAIN rerun.
Does not claim 50% slowest-query improvement.
summaries.title: Database Query Efficiency

caveats: Full E2E passed with retry artifact logs still recorded as flake follow-up context.
Web coverage is measured, but strict coverage thresholds remain intentionally unset.
summaries.title: Test Coverage and Quality

caveats: Process-level unhandledRejection and uncaughtException handlers are now covered as operational runtime hardening, not as one of the original three user-facing fixes.
summaries.title: Runtime Error and Edge Case Handling

caveats: Lighthouse was not rerun.
Automated axe does not prove full keyboard or screen-reader completion.
summaries.title: Accessibility Compliance

caveats: Remote/deployed destructive probing remains out of scope by default; remote mode requires explicit --allow-write and --allow-stress.
Created local probe records are retained unless a future run uses cleanup support.
Input sanitization probes sample the major current user-facing write/read surfaces and should be extended when new fields are introduced.
This is black-box plus assisted review evidence, not a formal external penetration test.
summaries.title: Security Audit
